steps.title: Find the main verb
steps.content: After the semicolon, the subject is "such symbiotic relationships." The inserted "wherein" clause modifies those relationships, so the sentence still needs a finite main verb to connect the subject to "extensive networks."

steps.title: Rule out nonfinite forms
steps.content: A, B, and C are nonfinite forms, so they cannot serve as the main verb of the independent clause after the semicolon. The plural subject needs a finite present-tense verb. The answer is D.
